global.title: The grid was melting down in last week’s heat – until EVs came to the rescue

global.title: Kalmar is building – and selling! – these massive electric reach stackers in China

global.title: CATL is building more than 200 battery swap stations EVERY MONTH
global.summary: The CATL-backed Choco-SEB battery swap network deployed its 2,000th swapping station June 30th, wrapping up another month of incredibly rapid growth that’s seen the company average more than 200 deployments in each and every month of 2026. (!)

global.title: GM is flooding dealers with Bolt EVs – is that part of the plan?
global.summary: GM raised plenty of eyebrows when it announced that its resurrected 2027 Chevrolet Bolt – one of the lowest-priced, most capable new EVs in North America – would only be built for a single model year. global.summary: The Washington, DC, metro area is getting its first en-route overhead pantograph electric bus chargers.

global.summary: In the Electrek Podcast, we discuss the most popular news in the world of sustainable transport and energy. In this week’s episode, we discuss the launch of the Tesla Model YL, BMW iX5, and EV sales results for the second quarter.
